title: BAI Prez Himanta Biswa Sarma on India Open Incident
indian_angle: The statement comes from the President of the Badminton Association of India regarding an incident at the India Open.
summary: BAI President Himanta Biswa Sarma commented on an unusual incident at the India Open, asking 'What is the problem if monkeys come to watch a game?'.

title: Indian Shuttlers Should Learn to Think on Their Feet Without a Coach
indian_angle: This article provides crucial advice for Indian shuttlers to improve their game by fostering self-reliance during matches.
summary: An opinion piece suggests that Indian shuttlers need to cultivate independent thinking and decision-making on court, rather than relying solely on coaches, to achieve greater success.

title: Badminton's 3x15 Scoring System to Debut in Domestic Tournaments
indian_angle: The Badminton Association of India is implementing a new 3x15 scoring system across all domestic ranking tournaments and national championships for U-13, U-15, and U-17 categories, starting in Ernakulam.
summary: The Badminton Association of India announced the implementation of a 3x15 scoring system for all domestic ranking tournaments and national championships in the U-13, U-15, and U-17 age groups, with its debut set for Ernakulam.

title: Kidambi Srikanth Reaches US Open Final, Shows Sportsmanship
indian_angle: Indian shuttler Kidambi Srikanth advanced to the US Open final, with a notable moment of sportsmanship highlighted where he overturned a point given to him.
summary: Kidambi Srikanth made it to the US Open final, with a report detailing his journey and a specific instance where he displayed exemplary sportsmanship by correcting a point awarded in his favor.

title: Ronaldo Fan Rounak Chouhan Defeats World No 6 at US Open
indian_angle: Indian shuttler Rounak Chouhan, a self-proclaimed Cristiano Ronaldo fan, caused a major upset by defeating World No 6 Chou Tien Chen at the US Open Super 300 tournament.
summary: Rounak Chouhan, an Indian shuttler, achieved a significant victory at the US Open Super 300, stunning the World No 6 Chou Tien Chen in a notable performance.

title: Thomas Cup Squad Reassembles for Asian Games: Treesa-Gayatri Return
indian_angle: The Indian badminton squad, including the return of Treesa Jolly and Gayatri Gopichand, is reassembling from the Thomas Cup for preparations for the upcoming Asian Games.
summary: The Indian badminton team, featuring players like PV Sindhu, Lakshya Sen, Unnati Hooda, Satwiksairaj Rankireddy, and Chirag Shetty, along with the return of Treesa Jolly and Gayatri Gopichand, is regrouping for the Asian Games after their Thomas Cup commitments.
